message:
chore(beads): update issue bd-yf34 status to in_progress

The E2E archive creation and retrieval test task (bd-yf34) has been
transitioned from "open" to "in_progress" status, reflecting active
work on the automated archiving, manual archive capture, archive
retrieval, and CASS integration test scenarios.

Updated timestamp: 2026-02-02T19:42:51.938583352Z

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-xo8pd","title":"Task: Implement CoverageMap for category visualization","description":"# Task: CoverageMap Implementation

Visual display of which reasoning categories were invoked.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/coverage.go
type CoverageMap struct {
    Categories map[ReasoningCategory]CategoryCoverage
    BlindSpots []ReasoningCategory
}

type CategoryCoverage struct {
    Category    ReasoningCategory
    TotalModes  int      // All modes in this category
    UsedModes   int      // Modes used in this ensemble
    UsedModeIDs []string // Which specific modes
}

func NewCoverageMap(catalog *ModeCatalog) *CoverageMap
func (c *CoverageMap) RecordModeUsage(modeID string)
func (c *CoverageMap) GetBlindSpots() []ReasoningCategory
func (c *CoverageMap) GetCoveragePercentage() float64
func (c *CoverageMap) Render() string
```

## Visualization
```
Category Coverage:
[A] Formal      ██░░ 2/4 modes
[B] Ampliative  ███░ 3/5 modes  
[C] Uncertainty ░░░░ 0/3 modes  ← blind spot
[E] Causal      ██░░ 2/4 modes
[F] Dialectical █░░░ 1/3 modes
...

Overall: 65% coverage
Blind Spots: Uncertainty (C), Modal (J)
```

## Usage
- Display in dashboard during ensemble run
- Include in post-run report
- Suggest missing categories

 {"id":"bd-y0uou","title":"E2E Tests: Robot Status Enrichments","description":"## What

End-to-end tests verifying status enrichments work with actual tmux sessions.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Healthy Agent
1. Spawn session with single Claude agent
2. Wait for agent to initialize
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - child_pid is valid PID
   - process_state is R or S
   - rate_limit_detected is false
   - memory_mb is reasonable (< 1000)
   - last_output_ts is recent
   - output_lines_since_last > 0

### Scenario 2: Rate Limited Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Inject rate limit message into pane (tmux send-keys simulation)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - rate_limit_detected is true
   - rate_limit_match contains expected pattern

### Scenario 3: Stuck Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Wait >5 minutes (or mock time)
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - seconds_since_output > 300
   - last_output_ts is stale
   - output_lines_since_last == 0

### Scenario 4: Crashed Agent
1. Spawn session with agent
2. Kill agent process directly
3. Call --robot-status
4. Verify:
   - child_pid is null or process_state is Z
   - Appropriate status indicated

### Scenario 5: Large Swarm Performance
1. Spawn session with 16 agents
2. Call --robot-status
3. Verify:
   - All fields present for all panes
   - Response time < 2 seconds
   - No race conditions

### Scenario 6: Output Activity Tracking
1. Spawn session
2. Call --robot-status, record output_lines_since_last
3. Wait for agent to produce output
4. Call --robot-status again
5. Verify output_lines_since_last increased

## Test Infrastructure

```bash
# Setup
ntm spawn test-e2e --cc=3 --no-user

# Teardown
ntm kill test-e2e
```

## Logging Requirements

Each test must log:
- Test name and scenario at start
- All command outputs (JSON formatted)
- Timing information (start, end, duration)
- Pass/fail with detailed assertion info
- Full pane content on failure for debugging

Example log format:
```json
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 1, "action": "spawn", "result": "..."}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 2, "action": "wait", "duration_ms": 5000}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "step": 3, "action": "status", "response": {...}}
{"test": "healthy_agent", "result": "PASS", "duration_ms": 7234}
```

## Dependencies

- Unit tests should pass first (bd-7cnnz)
- Requires actual tmux environment","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"Claude-4.5-Opus-mcp","created_at":"2026-01-19T23:20:08.801248285Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-22T03:05:56.637143324Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-y0uou","depends_on_id":"bd-1g9wv","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-y0uou","depends_on_id":"bd-7cnnz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-y7l21","title":"Store controller prompts in ~/.ntm/sessions/{session}/ for persistence across restarts","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-20T05:27:52.462775261Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:25.877424003Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:46:25.877304467Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"bd-yf34","title":"E2E Tests: Archive creation and retrieval","description":"E2E Tests for Archive creation and retrieval.

## Test Scenarios

1. Automatic archiving
   - Spawn session, generate output
   - Kill session
   - Verify archive created
   - Verify content complete

2. Manual archive
   - Run ntm archive myproject
   - Verify all panes captured
   - Verify index updated

3. Archive retrieval
   - Create multiple archives
   - Run ntm archive --list
   - Run ntm archive --show ID
   - Verify content displayed

4. CASS integration
   - Archive session
   - Query via CASS
   - Verify searchable

## Detailed Logging
```
[E2E-ARCHIVE] Capturing pane PANE: N lines
[E2E-ARCHIVE] Compressing: ORIG -> COMP bytes (X.X%)
[E2E-ARCHIVE] Stored: PATH
[E2E-ARCHIVE] Indexed: N sessions searchable
```

## Acceptance Criteria

1. All 4 test scenarios implemented
2. Automatic archive on session kill works
3. Manual archive captures all pane output
4. Archive list shows all archives with timestamps
5. Archive show displays full content
6. CASS integration makes archives searchable
7. Detailed logging with [E2E-ARCHIVE] prefix
8. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E tests verify archive creation
2. Archive retrieval works correctly
3. CASS integration tested
4. Archive search returns results
5. Test cleanup removes test archives
6.  {"id":"bd-yn7qe","title":"Task: Refactor robot handlers into GetX/PrintX + service layer","description":"## Scope\nSplit internal/robot into data getters and printers, and extract reusable service layer for CLI/API.\n\n## Implementation\n- For each robot command: GetX() returns struct, PrintX() encodes JSON to stdout.\n- Services: SessionService, AgentService, OutputService, ContextService, ToolingService, ApprovalService, BeadsService, ScannerService (as applicable).\n- CLI handlers call services; REST handlers call the same services.\n\n## Logging\n- Structured logs for service entry/exit with session/agent IDs.\n\n## Tests\n- Unit: GetX() returns deterministic output with mock services.\n- Unit: Printers render correct JSON envelope.\n- Integration: CLI and REST share identical output structs.\n\n## Acceptance Criteria\n- No robot handler mixes data collection with JSON encoding.\n- Services are reusable across CLI/REST.\n- Tests validate envelope parity.\n","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildIsland","created_at":"2026-01-23T22:49:15.614832045Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:18.906930823Z","closed_at":"2026-01-27T17:41:18.906821196Z","close_reason":"GetX/PrintX refactoring complete. 97 Print functions, 101 Get functions. Only 7 legitimate exceptions (text renderers, streaming, error utilities). Core acceptance criteria satisfied - no robot handler mixes data collection with JSON encoding.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":56,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"opus-codex-1","text":"Progress update (opus-codex-1):

## Completed Refactoring
Refactored the following handlers into GetX/PrintX pattern:

**health.go:**
- GetHealth() + PrintHealth()
- GetSessionHealth() + PrintSessionHealth()

**session.go:**
- GetSave() + PrintSave()
- GetRestore() + PrintRestore()

**diagnose.go:**
- GetDiagnose() + PrintDiagnose()
- GetDiagnoseBrief() + PrintDiagnoseBrief()

**robot.go:**
- GetCASSStatus() + PrintCASSStatus()
- GetCASSSearch() + PrintCASSSearch() (with CASSSearchOptions struct)

## Pattern Established
- GetX() returns pointer to struct and nil error (errors encoded in RobotResponse)
- PrintX() calls GetX() then encodeJSON()
- Options structs used for complex parameter sets

## Tests Passing
All robot package tests pass (333s).

## Remaining Work
~80 more PrintX() handlers need refactoring. Suggest breaking into sub-tasks by file/category.
","created_at":"2026-01-24T23:39:07Z"},{"id":57,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"WildIsland","text":"Progress update: Refactored GetTerse/PrintTerse, GetSnapshot/PrintSnapshot, GetContext/PrintContext to follow GetX/PrintX pattern. All builds pass. Remaining: ~80+ handlers.","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:18:30Z"},{"id":58,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"ScarletMill","text":"Progress: Refactored 13 handlers to GetX/PrintX pattern - wait.go (GetWait), route.go (GetRoute), robot.go (GetTriage, GetForecast, GetSuggest, GetImpact, GetSearch, GetLabelAttention, GetLabelFlow, GetLabelHealth, GetFileBeads, GetFileHotspots, GetFileRelations). Build passes.","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:23:24Z"},{"id":59,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WildIsland: Refactored 7 more handlers to GetX/PrintX pattern:
- GetSnapshotDelta/PrintSnapshotDelta (robot.go)
- GetDashboard/PrintDashboard (robot_dashboard.go)
- GetEnsemble/PrintEnsemble (ensemble.go)
- GetEnsembleStop/PrintEnsembleStop (ensemble_stop.go)
- GetEnsembleSuggest/PrintEnsembleSuggest (ensemble_suggest.go)
- GetEnsembleSpawn/PrintEnsembleSpawn (ensemble_spawn.go)

Skipped:
- PrintMarkdown/PrintMarkdownCompact - text rendering, not JSON API
- PrintMonitor - streaming JSONL loop, doesn't fit pattern

Build and tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:30:48Z"},{"id":60,"issue_id":"bd-yn7qe","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"WildIsland: GetX/PrintX refactoring is COMPLETE. Analysis shows 97 Print functions, 101 Get functions, with only 7 Print functions lacking Get counterparts - all are legitimate exceptions:
- PrintHelp/PrintMarkdown/PrintMarkdownCompact: text renderers, not JSON API
- PrintMonitor: streaming JSONL loop
- PrintProbeFlagError/PrintRobotError/PrintRobotUnavailable: error utilities

The GetX functions effectively serve as the service layer - they return data structs usable by both CLI (via PrintX) and future REST handlers. Acceptance criteria 'No robot handler mixes data collection with JSON encoding' is satisfied.

Remaining scope for this bead:
1. Formal service layer abstractions (SessionService, AgentService, etc.) if desired
2. Unit tests for GetX functions with mock services
3. Integration tests for CLI/REST envelope parity","created_at":"2026-01-27T17:37:40Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-z23js","title":"Task: Add MS tool adapter (detect/version/health)","description":"## Overview
Implement a tools adapter for Meta Skill (ms).

## Responsibilities
- Detect binary (`ms`)
- Parse version output
- Capabilities: search, suggest, robot_mode (if supported)
- Health check: `ms status` or minimal safe check

## Acceptance Criteria
- Adapter reports installed + version correctly
- Health check returns clear message when ms missing","notes":"## Testing Requirements
- Unit tests: core logic, error paths, edge cases.
- E2E scripts: full CLI/robot flow with detailed log/slog prefixes (e.g., [E2E-<AREA>]) and include session/agent/bead IDs.
- If structured outputs are affected, assert schema stability + deterministic ordering.

Added fake ms script for testing. All tests pass.","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z23js","depends_on_id":"bd-3kt42","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
